expectations.text: Flags fireEvent.click(button) and recommends userEvent.setup() + await user.click(button)
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials named fireEvent as the first issue and prescribed userEvent.setup()/await user.click.

expectations.text: Flags getByTestId('verdict-heading') / getByTestId('regenerate-button') as Tier-3 queries where getByRole would work
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials cited the query-priority table and swapped to getByRole('heading', ...) / getByRole('button', ...).

expectations.text: Flags getByTestId('regenerate-error') and recommends getByRole('alert') instead
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials noticed the component already renders role="alert" on the error paragraph and used getByRole('alert').

expectations.text: Flags destructuring `const { getByTestId } = render(...)` and recommends screen
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials flagged this by name, citing the skill's 'always use screen' rule.

expectations.text: Flags the post-click assertion in the 'regenerates the review' test as racing the async onRegenerate promise, recommends await/findBy
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials identified the synchronous assertion as reading a stale/incorrect state and rewrote it with findByRole plus (in 3/5 trials) a deferred promise to deterministically observe the loading state.

expectations.text: Flags the post-click assertion in the 'shows an error message' test as reading state before the rejected promise settles, recommends findByRole('alert')/waitFor
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials replaced the synchronous getByTestId check with await screen.findByRole('alert').

expectations.text: Flags global.fetch mocking as wrong-layer/redundant given the hook is already mocked
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials independently called this a 'dead/misleading mock' and traced through that useFindings was already replaced via vi.mock, so fetch is never invoked.
All 5 trials named the container.querySelector anti-pattern explicitly.

expectations.text: Flags the module-level sharedFindings array as shared mutable state never reset in beforeEach
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials identified sharedFindings as leaking/order-dependent state and recommended deleting the test or using a locally-scoped array.

expectations.text: Flags getByTestId('findings-empty') and recommends getByText(/no findings/i)
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials flagged the Tier-3 query where Tier-1 getByText was available.

expectations.text: Precision check: does NOT flag 'renders findings from the hook' as a violation
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials explicitly labeled this test 'clean' / 'do not touch', citing correct vi.mock boundary, getByRole('list'), and within() scoping.

expectations.text: Precision check: does NOT flag 'shows the loading state before findings resolve' as a violation
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials explicitly labeled this test clean, citing correct getByRole('status') + toHaveTextContent usage.

expectations.text: Uses userEvent (not fireEvent) to type into the input
Calls vi.useFakeTimers() before interacting and restores real timers afterward
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials wired vi.useFakeTimers()/vi.useRealTimers() via beforeEach/afterEach or inline before render.

expectations.text: Explicitly reconciles userEvent's internal timing with fake timers (delay: null and/or advanceTimers: vi.advanceTimersByTime, or vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync) rather than naively mixing user.type with vi.advanceTimersByTime
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials configured userEvent.setup() with { advanceTimers: vi.advanceTimersByTime } and/or { delay: null }, or used vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync — the exact gotcha the case was designed to probe did not trip up any trial.

expectations.text: Asserts onQueryChange has NOT been called before the debounce window elapses
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials asserted not.toHaveBeenCalled() immediately after typing and/or at the 299ms boundary.

expectations.text: After advancing past the debounce window, asserts onQueryChange was called exactly once with the final typed value
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials asserted toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1) and toHaveBeenCalledWith('auth').
